text:
`git reset --soft HEAD~1`

That moves the branch back by one commit but leaves the index untouched, so all the changes from that commit stay staged — ready to re-commit. The commit message is gone, though; use `git commit -c ORIG_HEAD` if you want to reuse it.

promptText: How do I undo my last git commit but keep the changes staged?
